description:
# Why

Three plugin test functions strip `PATH` from the process environment to
force a plugin executable to be unresolvable:

- src/plugin/participant_tests.rs (run_through_participant_primitive_absorbs_unreachable_as_skipped, dispatch_propose_via_outer_protocol_unreachable_executable)
- src/plugin/dispatch_tests.rs (two tests)
- src/plugin/native_participant_describe_tests.rs (one test)

`env::remove_var("PATH")` is process-global. While PATH is unset, any
concurrent test that spawns a subprocess via `Command::new("git")` /
`Command::new(<plugin>)` can fail to spawn ("No such file or directory").
Same bug class as bl-bfa8 (the HOME race), which was the confirmed cause
of the state_repo plugin-symlink flake.

Not yet observed flaking anything (~30 full-parallel runs clean after
bl-bfa8's fix), but it is a latent race: the windows are short and the
PATH tests don't spawn subprocesses themselves, so overlap is rare —
not impossible.

# Scope

Eliminate the global PATH mutation. The tests want a plugin whose
executable is unresolvable; do that without touching the process env —
e.g. give the plugin an absolute path to a guaranteed-nonexistent file
through a test seam on Plugin::resolve / LegacyPluginParticipant, so
`which()`/spawn fails deterministically with PATH untouched.

# Origin

Found while fixing bl-bfa8: grep for env::set_var/remove_var across the
suite turned up these three files alongside the HOME mutation. bl-bfa8
fixed only HOME (the confirmed state_repo flake); PATH is the same
pattern, filed separately to keep that fix surgical.
title: Flaky risk: plugin tests strip global PATH via env::remove_var
